summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of WhatToEat pregnancy nutrition app. All core features working correctly including: food grid display, client-side search filtering, category/safety filters, bottom navigation, settings page with dietary restrictions, dietary concern warnings on food cards and in modals, food detail modal with all sections (Nutritional Benefits, Recommended Consumption, Preparation Tips, Precautions, Allergy Warning, View References, Educational Disclaimer), and localStorage persistence.

issues: On mobile viewport (390x844), the 'Made with Emergent' badge overlaps the Settings nav item in the bottom navigation, making it difficult to click. This is an Emergent badge issue, not app code issue - can be ignored.

passed_tests: Home page loads with food grid displaying 40 foods
Search input filters foods client-side instantly (apple search returns 1 result)
Category filter works - Fruits: 10 foods, Proteins, Dairy, etc.
Safety level filter works - Safe: 34 foods, Limit: 3 foods, Avoid: 3 foods
Bottom navigation bar appears with Home, FAQ, Topics, About, Settings
Settings page accessible via bottom nav
All 10 dietary restrictions options visible (Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Nut-Free, Shellfish-Free, Egg-Free, Soy-Free, Low Sodium, Diabetic Friendly)
Save Preferences button saves dietary restrictions to localStorage
Active restrictions indicator shows on home page when restrictions are set
Food cards show dietary concern warnings (e.g., Chicken shows 'Contains meat/fish' for vegetarian)
Greek Yogurt shows 'Contains dairy' warning for dairy-free restriction
Food detail modal opens when clicking a food card
Modal shows: Back to Home button, Share button, food name, category, safety badge
Modal displays Nutritional Benefits section with tags
Modal displays Recommended Consumption section with checkmarks
Modal displays Preparation Tips section with checkmarks
Modal displays Precautions section with red background
Modal displays Allergy Warning when applicable (tested with Almonds)
Modal displays View References collapsible section
Modal shows Dietary Alert when food conflicts with user's dietary restrictions
Educational disclaimer appears at bottom of modal
localStorage persistence works - restrictions persist after page refresh
Mobile responsiveness - app works on mobile viewport
All backend APIs working: /api/health, /api/foods/all, /api/foods/search, /api/foods/{id}, /api/categories, /api/safety-levels
seed_data_creation: None required - app uses LOCAL_FOODS static data in backend
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core features tested and working. The app uses localStorage for dietary restrictions persistence. Food data is served from a static LOCAL_FOODS array in server.py (no database dependency for food data). The Emergent badge at bottom of page can sometimes overlap the Settings nav item on very small mobile screens, but this is not an app code issue.
